channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: New Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth on priorities, vision for 2025 session
item.description: Under a new power-sharing deal, Demuth will be speaker at least through next year. She spoke with MPR News host Cathy Wurzer.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, Ngoc Bui, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: Musk team's access to student loan systems raises alarms over personal information for millions
item.description: Democrats are opposing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising questions about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conservative firebrand 'considering all possibilities' for two key races in Georgia next year
item.description: Georgia Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, a top Trump ally in the House and one of the best known members of Congress, keeps the door wide open to possible runs for Senate or governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin appeals ruling blocking birthright citizenship order
item.description: The Trump administration appealed a Washington federal judge’s order blocking the president’s executive order (EO) on birthright citizenship from being enforced, hours after the judge scolded the administration for using the EO for personal and political gains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers denied entry into Department of Education
item.description: The doors were locked, and a security guard could be seen having a tense conversation with a lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: GM, Pepsi, Disney, 3M, others scrub some DEI references from investor reports
item.description: Some companies have announced diversity rollbacks — but many more are deleting or softening language from their investor disclosures, an NPR analysis finds.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Indonesia orders the halting of tourism projects linked to Trump over environmental issues
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian authorities have ordered the halting of development of a tourism project affiliated with U.S. President Donald Trump over water management and environmental issues, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor's Race Poll: Cuomo the favorite, Adams looking stronger
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An exclusive poll from PIX11 News, Emerson College, and The Hill shows former Gov. Andrew Cuomo would hold an early advantage if he hops in the New York City Democratic mayoral primary — but Mayor Eric Adams still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: John Fetterman reveals how he'll vote on Trump's Tulsi Gabbard and RFK Jr. nominations
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman announced that he will vote against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard to serve as DNI and against confirming Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to serve as HHS secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP rushing to produce Trump's big budget bill with tax cuts, program cuts and other promises
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans are working overtime after a lengthy White House meeting to meet President Donald Trump's demand for a big budget package that includes some $3 trillion in tax breaks, massive program cuts and a possible extension of the nation's debt limit.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada consumers could get a break on egg prices
item.description: Just about everyone buys eggs, and higher prices haven't gone unnoticed in Carson City, where a law passed in 2021 is partly responsible for the eggflation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Member of Elon Musk’s DOGE team resigns after racist posts resurface
item.description: Marko Elez had recently been given special access by a federal judge to highly sensitive payment systems in the Department of Treasury.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Joe Rogan cheers Musk’s ‘business approach’ to government cuts
item.description: “What’s fascinating right now is we’re getting a chance to see what happens when you take a business approach to the government,” Rogan said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers need more time on buyout decision: Money expert
item.description: Millions of U.S. government employees must make a decision whether to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia lawmakers advance a bill to prevent transgender women from competing in women's sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A day after Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stood with President Donald Trump in Washington as he signed an executive order intended to prevent transgender athletes from competing in women's sports, he returned to Georgia where state Senate Republicans made a similar move.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'Iron Dome for America' a major undertaking: Newton
item.description: A proposed $2.5 trillion "Iron Dome for America" is expected to be much more complex than its Israel-based counterpart
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota distillery alarmed by now-delayed Canadian tariffs, retaliatory measures
item.description: The Princeton-based company exports about 5 percent of its products, including vodkas, brandies and liqueurs, to Canada. There, the company’s Butter Ripple Schnapps is used in a drink named after American actor Burt Reynolds.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 60 Minutes publishes transcript of Harris interview amid FCC investigation
item.description: "We are posting the same transcripts and videos of our interview with Vice President Kamala Harris that we provided to the FCC," the news program wrote on its website.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: State lawmaker behind ‘trans refuge’ law blasts Trump order on trans athletes in school
item.description: Rep. Leigh Finke, DFL-St. Paul, says the order is saying that transgender people should not be allowed to be active members of society.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Nevada highway projects stuck as 90-day review plays out; Brightline effects uncertain
item.description: Nevada is waiting for a sign from the federal government: Can we count on funding or not? "So, we have pulled in a lot of what we are doing," NDOT Director Tracy Larkin Thomason told lawmakers on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada prisons director points to 4-mile fence, better staffing; inmate food issues persist
item.description: A high-profile inmate escape, the resignation of the prisons director, and reports that fences, doors and windows were vulnerable. It all added up to a spotlight on the Nevada Department of Corrections in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. appears on track to become US health secretary as he wins key Republican senator's support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal vaccine skeptic and activist lawyer, appeared on track to become the nation's health secretary after winning the crucial support of Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration's push for a federal funding freeze is back in court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned Trump administration freeze on federal funding is heading back to a Washington courtroom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Florida immigration bill, marijuana petition spark debate
item.description: The battle between Gov. Ron DeSantis and state GOP lawmakers has become more public and continues to heat up.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC
